    The day_trade_buying_power_issued_amount is the day trade buying power of the account in USD, returned from the request.
    boolean
    equals(Object o)
     
    int
    hashCode()
     
    Optional<Boolean>
    isDayTradeBuyingPowerAllowed()
    The is_day_trade_buying_power_allowed boolean will be true if the account is a Margin account, PDT is true and SOD Account equity >= $25,000, otherwise it will be false.
    Optional<String>
    name()
    The service generated name of the BuyingPower Format: accounts/{account_id}/buyingPower

  • Constructor Details

    • BuyingPower

      public BuyingPower(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AccountEquityAmount> accountEquityAmount, Optional<String> accountId, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends BuyingPowerAmount> buyingPowerAmount, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends DayTradeBuyingPowerAmount> dayTradeBuyingPowerAmount, Optional<Boolean> isDayTradeBuyingPowerAllowed, Optional<String> name, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PositionMarketValueAmount> positionMarketValueAmount, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends Requirements> requirements, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends SmaAmount> smaAmount)

    • BuyingPower

      public BuyingPower()

  • Method Details

    • accountEquityAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<AccountEquityAmount> accountEquityAmount()
      The account_equity in USD returned from the request.

    • accountId

      public Optional<String> accountId()
      The account ID returned from the request.

    • buyingPowerAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<BuyingPowerAmount> buyingPowerAmount()
      The buying_power of the account in USD returned from the request.

    • dayTradeBuyingPowerAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<DayTradeBuyingPowerAmount> dayTradeBuyingPowerAmount()
      The day_trade_buying_power_issued_amount is the day trade buying power of the account in USD, returned from the request. If the is_day_trade_buying_power_allowed boolean is true this will be set from day_trade_buying_power_issued value returned from the margins calculator, or else if it is false it will be set from buying_power_issued value returned from the margins calculator.

    • isDayTradeBuyingPowerAllowed

      public Optional<Boolean> isDayTradeBuyingPowerAllowed()
      The is_day_trade_buying_power_allowed boolean will be true if the account is a Margin account, PDT is true and SOD Account equity >= $25,000, otherwise it will be false.

    • name

      public Optional<String> name()
      The service generated name of the BuyingPower Format: accounts/{account_id}/buyingPower

    • positionMarketValueAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PositionMarketValueAmount> positionMarketValueAmount()
      The position_market_value in USD returned from the request.

    • requirements

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Requirements> requirements()
      Margin Requirements fields returned from the request.

    • smaAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<SmaAmount> smaAmount()
      The sma_amount is the special memorandum account amount in USD, returned from the request. This will only be populated for margin accounts and is the margin equity minus the RegT requirements.

    • builder

      public static BuyingPower.Builder builder()

    • withAccountEquityAmount

      public BuyingPower withAccountEquityAmount(AccountEquityAmount accountEquityAmount)
      The account_equity in USD returned from the request.

      public BuyingPower withBuyingPowerAmount(BuyingPowerAmount buyingPowerAmount)
      The buying_power of the account in USD returned from the request.

    • withBuyingPowerAmount

      public BuyingPower withBuyingPowerAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends BuyingPowerAmount> buyingPowerAmount)
      The buying_power of the account in USD returned from the request.

    • withDayTradeBuyingPowerAmount

      public BuyingPower withDayTradeBuyingPowerAmount(DayTradeBuyingPowerAmount dayTradeBuyingPowerAmount)
      The day_trade_buying_power_issued_amount is the day trade buying power of the account in USD, returned from the request. If the is_day_trade_buying_power_allowed boolean is true this will be set from day_trade_buying_power_issued value returned from the margins calculator, or else if it is false it will be set from buying_power_issued value returned from the margins calculator.

    • withDayTradeBuyingPowerAmount

      public BuyingPower withDayTradeBuyingPowerAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends DayTradeBuyingPowerAmount> dayTradeBuyingPowerAmount)
      The day_trade_buying_power_issued_amount is the day trade buying power of the account in USD, returned from the request. If the is_day_trade_buying_power_allowed boolean is true this will be set from day_trade_buying_power_issued value returned from the margins calculator, or else if it is false it will be set from buying_power_issued value returned from the margins calculator.

    • withIsDayTradeBuyingPowerAllowed

      public BuyingPower withIsDayTradeBuyingPowerAllowed(boolean isDayTradeBuyingPowerAllowed)
      The is_day_trade_buying_power_allowed boolean will be true if the account is a Margin account, PDT is true and SOD Account equity >= $25,000, otherwise it will be false.

    • withIsDayTradeBuyingPowerAllowed

      public BuyingPower withIsDayTradeBuyingPowerAllowed(Optional<Boolean> isDayTradeBuyingPowerAllowed)
      The is_day_trade_buying_power_allowed boolean will be true if the account is a Margin account, PDT is true and SOD Account equity >= $25,000, otherwise it will be false.

    • withSmaAmount

      public BuyingPower withSmaAmount(SmaAmount smaAmount)
      The sma_amount is the special memorandum account amount in USD, returned from the request. This will only be populated for margin accounts and is the margin equity minus the RegT requirements.

      The sma_amount is the special memorandum account amount in USD, returned from the request. This will only be populated for margin accounts and is the margin equity minus the RegT requirements.
